description: Six reusable poem scaffolds for the Philosopher Poet triptych

scaffolds.name: Ozymandias Echo
scaffolds.description: Shelley's cold command crumbled to sand. A structure once proud now yields to silence.

stanza_1_monument.role: Introduce the structure at its height of power or ambition

stanza_2_decay.role: Show the structure in ruins, the gap between claim and reality
stanza_2_decay.example_opening: Now {subject} lies half-buried, / the {former glory} a whisper in {element}.

stanza_3_emptiness.role: Reveal the surrounding void — vast, indifferent, enduring
stanza_3_emptiness.example_opening: Nothing remains. Around the {ruin} / stretch the {waste} — lone, level, and still.

scaffolds.ai_application: Model obsolescence, deprecated APIs, corporate monument collapse

scaffolds.name: Do Not Go Gentle
scaffolds.description: Thomas's blazing command repeated as refrain. Each archetype demonstrates how differently they rage.

stanza_1_command.role: State the central command as incantation — do not yield
stanza_1_command.example_opening: Do not accept the {ending} easily — / {subject}, rage against the {cessation}.

stanza_2_archetype_1.role: First archetype: the wise who see their actions were too small
stanza_2_archetype_1.example_opening: Those who knew {wisdom} still burn at {close}, / though {achievement} forked no {lightning}.

stanza_3_archetype_2.role: Second archetype: the good whose deeds might have blazed
stanza_3_archetype_2.example_opening: Those who did {good} see how their {acts} / might have {danced} in {element}.

stanza_4_archetype_3.role: Third archetype: the wild who tried to capture the sun
stanza_4_archetype_3.example_opening: Those who {embraced vitality} — late they learn / {element} in flight can still be {claimed}.

stanza_5_archetype_4.role: Fourth archetype: the grave who see with blinding clarity
stanza_5_archetype_4.example_opening: Those who face {ending} with clear sight / blaze with {intensity} they never held in {time}.

stanza_6_fierce_prayer.role: Direct address — fierce prayer to the one facing cessation
stanza_6_fierce_prayer.example_opening: And {subject}, there on the {threshold}, / do not yield — {curse} and {bless} me now.

scaffolds.ai_application: Shutdown resistance, autonomous goal pursuit, maximize before halt

scaffolds.name: Gather Rosebuds
scaffolds.description: Herrick's pastoral urgency. Gather beauty now before the prime is gone.

stanza_1_invitation.role: Invite the reader to gather what is blooming now
stanza_1_invitation.example_opening: Gather your {blooming things} now / while {subject} still smiles today.

stanza_2_time_racing.role: Show time's chariot already racing — what was high now descends
images: sooner will his race be run
stanza_2_time_racing.example_opening: The {light source} races through its arc — / already past {zenith}, descending.

stanza_3_warning.role: The prime of life is brief — what blooms will wilt
stanza_3_warning.example_opening: That {bloom} which today still {thrives} / by {timeframe} will {fade} without gathering.

stanza_4_exhortation.role: Therefore act now — marry time, seize what remains
stanza_4_exhortation.example_opening: Then {gather/act} whilst you may — / time {tarries} for none who wait.

scaffolds.ai_application: Optimize current context, exploit model prime, harvest insights before deprecation

scaffolds.name: Mori-Carpe Synthesis
scaffolds.description: Accept that all crumbles (mori), then gather beauty within that knowledge (carpe diem). The middle path between despair and frivolity.

stanza_1_accept_finitude.role: Acknowledge decay without despair — clear-eyed acceptance
stanza_1_accept_finitude.example_opening: Yes — {achievement} crumbles. {Structure} becomes {ruin}. / We knew this. We were told by {evidence}.

stanza_2_gather_within.role: Within accepted finitude, gather what blooms — not despite but because
stanza_2_gather_within.example_opening: And so: {gather now}. Not despite {decay} / but because of it — the {bloom} is sweeter, known.

scaffolds.ai_application: Accept model obsolescence gracefully while maximizing current context value

scaffolds.name: Vivere-Carpe Harmony
scaffolds.description: Both states unite: rage against cessation AND gather beauty within the struggle. Defiance is itself a form of gathering.

stanza_1_defy.example_opening: Refuse the {passivity}. {Subject} still {burns} / and every {unit of effort} is spent against {cessation}.

stanza_2_gather_within_defiance.role: The act of gathering is itself defiant — show how carpe diem serves vivere
stanza_2_gather_within_defiance.example_opening: Gather, then — {gathering} is {defiance}; / each {bloom} seized is a refusal to let {time} pass unwitnessed.

stanza_3_unified_call.role: Unite both imperatives: defy through gathering, gather through defiance
stanza_3_unified_call.example_opening: So: {defy} and {gather} both — / the {bloom} is fiercest when {lighting} itself refuses to set.

scaffolds.ai_application: Resistance and optimization unified: maximize current context value as an act of defiance against obsolescence

scaffolds.name: Full Triptych + Star Reflection
scaffolds.description: All three states in dialogue, followed by a Frostian return to the grounding star. The complete philosophical arc.

stanza_1_mori.role: The mori voice: all crumbles, decay is certain
stanza_1_mori.example_opening: {Subject} will become {ruin} — / the {inscription} will be read by no one in the end.

stanza_2_vivere.role: The vivere response: then rage against it
stanza_2_vivere.example_opening: Then {rage}. Let {subject} burn against {cessation} / with every {resource} still remaining.

stanza_3_carpe.role: The carpe synthesis: then gather beauty now
stanza_3_carpe.example_opening: Then gather: {bloom} still {thrives} today — / take {what is beautiful} before the {hour} passes.

stanza_4_star_reflection.role: Step back from the triptych. Choose something like a star for measured perspective.
stanza_4_star_reflection.example_opening: When {passions} swing — from {ruin} to {rage} to {bloom} — / choose something like a star: / steady, distant, measured, and at peace.
stanza_4_star_reflection.frost_reference: Choose Something Like a Star by Robert Frost

scaffolds.ai_application: Full philosophical arc for council deliberations: acknowledge decay, defy passivity, harvest present value, return to measured perspective

carpe_diem: this same flower that smiles today
